data_in.description: Salmon quantification output files (quant.sf) from tximportData package
Transcript-to-gene mapping data.frame (tx2gene) with transcript IDs and corresponding gene IDs

data_out.description: Gene-level count matrix from two-step workflow (transcript import + summarizeToGene)
Gene-level count matrix from direct single-step import (txOut=FALSE)
Boolean confirmation that both matrices are identical

domain_knowledge: The txOut parameter controls whether tximport returns transcript-level (txOut=TRUE) or gene-level (txOut=FALSE, default) matrices; both workflows should produce identical gene-level counts when summarizeToGene is applied downstream.
A tx2gene data.frame must contain at minimum two columns: transcript identifiers (matching the row names of the quantification output) and corresponding gene identifiers for proper aggregation.
Gene-level summarization collapses transcript-level abundance, counts, and length estimates; the length matrix is particularly important as it accounts for differential isoform usage across samples.
Salmon quantification files (quant.sf) contain per-transcript estimates of abundance (TPM), estimated counts, and effective transcript length; tximport reads and structures these into matrix form.

direct_checks: verify file exists: tximportData salmon quantification files (input directory from github:thelovelab__tximport)
verify file exists: tx2gene mapping object (tx2gene data.frame from tximportData or article SI)
script_runs: execute tximport() with txOut=TRUE on salmon files and store result
script_runs: execute summarizeToGene() on txOut=TRUE result using tx2gene mapping
script_runs: execute tximport() with txOut=FALSE (default) on same salmon files

output_matches_reference: verify count matrix from (txOut=TRUE + summarizeToGene) is byte-for-byte identical to count matrix from txOut=FALSE, robust to floating-point representation choices in matrix serialization
file_format_is: both resulting count matrices are in standard R matrix or SummarizedExperiment format
expert_review: verify that the tximport parameters (type, countsFromAbundance, etc.) match those used in the article's reported equivalence test
assess whether numerical precision and rounding in the two pipelines are sufficiently aligned to justify claimed equivalence

evidence_snippets.text: [results] Do transcript-level estimates obtained via tximport with txOut=TRUE produce identical gene-level count matrices after summarization with summarizeToGene as compared to direct gene-level import using txOut=FALSE?: 'These matrices can then be summarized afterwards using the function `summarizeToGene`. This then gives the identical list of matrices as using `txOut=FALSE` (default) in the first `tximport` call.'

[results] Gene-level count matrices derived from transcript-level tximport output (txOut=TRUE) followed by summarizeToGene are identical to those produced by direct gene-level tximport (txOut=FALSE), as verified by all.equal() comparison.: 'all.equal(txi$counts, txi.sum$counts)'
[methods] Salmon quantification output files (quant.sf) from tximportData package: 'tximportData salmon files'
[intro] Transcript-to-gene mapping data.frame (tx2gene) with transcript IDs and corresponding gene IDs: 'Transcripts need to be associated with gene IDs for gene-level summarization'
[methods] Gene-level count matrix from two-step workflow (transcript import + summarizeToGene): 'resulting count matrix is identical to a direct tximport call'
[methods] Gene-level count matrix from direct single-step import (txOut=FALSE): 'direct tximport call with txOut=FALSE'
[methods] Boolean confirmation that both matrices are identical: 'verify the resulting count matrix is identical'
[intro] readr: 'significantly faster to read in files using the readr package'
[discussion] No changelog or version history provided for tximport package or source repository: '_No changelog found._'

cmd:
library(tximport); library(readr); salmon_files <- list.files('{inputs.salmon_quant_files}', pattern='quant.sf', full.names=TRUE); tx2gene <- read_csv('{inputs.tx2gene_mapping}'); txOut_true <- tximport(salmon_files, type='salmon', txOut=TRUE, tx2gene=tx2gene); gene_level_two_step <- summarizeToGene(txOut_true); txOut_false <- tximport(salmon_files, type='salmon', txOut=FALSE, tx2gene=tx2gene); are_identical <- all.equal(gene_level_two_step$abundance, txOut_false$abundance); cat('Gene-level matrices identical:', are_identical, '
'); saveRDS(list(two_step=gene_level_two_step$abundance, direct=txOut_false$abundance, identical=isTRUE(are_identical)), 'tximport_equivalence_result_{task_id}.rds')

finding: Gene-level count matrices derived from transcript-level tximport output (txOut=TRUE) followed by summarizeToGene are identical to those produced by direct gene-level tximport (txOut=FALSE), as verified by all.equal() comparison.
inputs: Salmon quantification output files (quant.sf) from tximportData package
Transcript-to-gene mapping data.frame (tx2gene) with transcript IDs and corresponding gene IDs
methodology_summary: Load salmon quantification files and tx2gene mapping, then import with tximport (type='salmon', txOut=TRUE) to obtain transcript-level matrices.
Apply summarizeToGene to collapse transcript-level counts to gene level using the tx2gene mapping.
Separately import the same salmon files with tximport (txOut=FALSE, default) to generate gene-level matrices directly.
Compare gene-level count matrices from both routes using all-equal or matrix-equality check.
Validation: Report success when both gene-level count matrices are bit-for-bit identical.

research_question: Do transcript-level estimates obtained via tximport with txOut=TRUE produce identical gene-level count matrices after summarization with summarizeToGene as compared to direct gene-level import using txOut=FALSE?

task_description: Run tximport on salmon transcript-abundance files with txOut=TRUE to obtain transcript-level counts, then apply summarizeToGene with a tx2gene mapping to collapse to gene level, and verify the resulting gene-level count matrix is identical to direct import with txOut=FALSE.
task_objective: Execute tximport with txOut=TRUE on salmon quantification files, apply summarizeToGene, and verify count matrix equivalence to direct gene-level tximport output.

task_outputs.label: Gene-level counts from transcript import + summarizeToGene

title: Reproduce equivalence of txOut=TRUE followed by summarizeToGene versus txOut=FALSE in tximport
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: readr, Gene-level count matrix from two-step workflow (transcript import + summarizeToGene), Gene-level count matrix from direct single-step import (txOut=FALSE), Boolean confirmation that both matrices are identical.
workflow_description: 1. Load salmon quantification output files and a tx2gene data.frame (transcript-to-gene mapping) using tximport with type='salmon' and txOut=TRUE to retain transcript-level abundance, counts, and length matrices. 2. Apply summarizeToGene function to the transcript-level output, passing the tx2gene mapping to collapse transcript estimates to gene level. 3. Load the same salmon files directly using tximport with txOut=FALSE (default) to generate gene-level matrices in a single step. 4. Compare the gene-level count matrices from steps 2 and 3 to verify they are identical.
